title: L'Xing

{{Quote|Besides, master, quite frankly, the Gu-vandi and L'Xing needed a good war. They were races that relied more on words than actions, and a good, brisk killing woke them from their torpid state.|HK-47, to the Jedi Exile|audio=HK-47TheirTorpidState.flac|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
The '''L'Xing''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that lived on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Praven Prime]], which was a part of [[Gu-vandi]]-held [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. At some point by [[3951 BBY]], an [[HK-50 series assassin droid]] posing as a [[Translator droid/Legends|translator]] intentionally mistranslated a [[L'Xing (language)|L'Xing]] [[Language/Legends|language]] phrase, causing a long [[Gu-vandi Collective–L'Xing civil war|civil war]] between the L'Xing and the [[Gu-vandi Collective]]. As a result of the conflict, the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] withdrew its presence from Gu-vandi space, and the [[HK-series assassin droid]] [[HK-47]] eventually predicted that the war would drastically reduce the L'Xing population.

==Society and culture==
The L'Xing were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that lived on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Praven Prime]]<ref name="KOTOR2" /> in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Quelii sector/Legends|Quelii sector]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Praven Prime system/Legends|Praven Prime system]]</ref> They spoke the [[L'Xing (language)|L'Xing]] [[Language/Legends|language]], and, according to the [[HK-series assassin droid]] [[HK-47]], the species had a "torpid" disposition that led its members to favor words over actions.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

==History==
===A war instigated===
{{Quote|Recitation: For example, on Praven Prime, the simple transferring of L'Xing syntax for 'friendship' changes its meaning—and implies that one's brood mate was actually impregnated by their own host. ''[…]'' Statement: This comment, of course, caused a civil war between the Gu-vandi Collective and L'Xing that still persists to the current date.|HK-47|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
[[File:PravenPrime-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The L'Xing lived on Praven Prime.]]
By [[3959 BBY]], Praven Prime was a part of the "civilized [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]," the territory that had been widely [[Scout/Legends|explored]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Praven Prime system/Legends|Praven Prime system]]</ref> By some point between [[3955 BBY]] and [[3951 BBY]],<ref name="Dates">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', which establishes that the [[Gu-vandi Collective–L'Xing civil war|civil war]] between the [[Gu-vandi Collective]] and the L'Xing broke out no more than four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, to [[3951 BBY]].</ref> the L'Xing [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was situated in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] controlled by the [[Gu-vandi]] species, and it was also a part of the Republic. At one point, an [[HK-50 series assassin droid]]<ref name="KOTOR2" />—a unit of an [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] model that had originally been designed by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]] and that was later [[HK Manufacturing Plant|mass-produced]] by the rogue [[G0-T0 infrastructure planning system]] known as [[G0-T0]]<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>—masquerading as a [[Translator droid/Legends|translator droid]] intentionally mistranslated the L'Xing word for "friendship" to instead convey the implication that a specific individual's [[Reproduction/Legends|brood]] mate had been impregnated by their own host. The mistranslated phrase led to an outbreak of a [[Gu-vandi Collective–L'Xing civil war|civil war]] between the L'Xing and the [[Gu-vandi Collective]]. As a result of the civil war, the Republic decided to withdraw control over Praven Prime and retreated from Gu'vandi space.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

===A cold analysis===
{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}}
{{Quote|Also, the result of the war will be that the drastically reduced L'Xing population will eventually need to call upon the Republic for emergency relief.|HK-47|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
[[File:HK-47.png|thumb|right|250px|HK-47 (pictured) claimed the civil war had a positive impact on the L'Xing.]]
In 3951 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="Dates" /> while the civil war was still ongoing, HK-47,<ref name="KOTOR2" /> who had originally served as the basis for the HK-50 model,<ref name="KOTORCG" /> informed the Human [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] known as the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]] about the conflict involving the L'Xing. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] reasoned that the outbreak of the conflict and the withdrawal of the Republic would be eventually prove to be beneficial to that government, speculating that continued control over the world would have been an unnecessary burden in the form of resources needed to maintain [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] and [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] relations.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

HK-47 further claimed that the L'Xing had "needed a good war" and that the conflict had "woken" the species from its relatively inactive disposition. The droid predicted that the civil war would drastically reduce the L'Xing population, which would then be forced to petition the Republic to supply emergency relief. Lastly, HK-47 also prognosticated that the requested aid would only be granted four to five [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the onset of the war, and that in the meantime "[one] would have to be patient and let death and destruction run its course."<ref name="KOTOR2" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The L'Xing were introduced in the [[2004]] [[Obsidian Entertainment]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. In the game, the species can be mentioned in an optional conversation the player character, the Jedi Exile, can have with the character HK-47. This article assumes one hundred percent game completion and that the conversation took place as described.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{1stm}}
